verses.text: None speaking by the Spirit calls Jesus accursed; 'no man can say that Jesus is the Lord, but by the Holy Ghost.' The Spirit's mark. True confession of Christ is the Spirit's work.
None speaking by the Spirit calls Jesus accursed; 'no man can say that Jesus is the Lord, but by the Holy Ghost.' The Spirit's mark. True confession of Christ is the Spirit's work.
None speaking by the Spirit calls Jesus accursed; 'no man can say that Jesus is the Lord, but by the Holy Ghost.' The Spirit's mark. True confession of Christ is the Spirit's work.
Diversities of gifts, 'but the same Spirit'; the same Lord and God works all. One Giver. Varied gifts flow from one God.
Diversities of gifts, 'but the same Spirit'; the same Lord and God works all. One Giver. Varied gifts flow from one God.
Diversities of gifts, 'but the same Spirit'; the same Lord and God works all. One Giver. Varied gifts flow from one God.
To each is given a gift 'to profit withal' — wisdom, healing, tongues — all by the one Spirit. For the common good. Gifts are given to serve, not to exalt.

As the body is one with many members, so is Christ; 'by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body.' One body. All believers are joined in Christ.
As the body is one with many members, so is Christ; 'by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body.' One body. All believers are joined in Christ.
The body is not one member; if the foot say, I am not the hand, is it not of the body? Each belongs. None may exclude himself from the body.
The body is not one member; if the foot say, I am not the hand, is it not of the body? Each belongs. None may exclude himself from the body.
The body is not one member; if the foot say, I am not the hand, is it not of the body? Each belongs. None may exclude himself from the body.
The body is not one member; if the foot say, I am not the hand, is it not of the body? Each belongs. None may exclude himself from the body.
God set the members 'as it hath pleased him'; many members, yet one body. God's design. He places each where He wills.
God set the members 'as it hath pleased him'; many members, yet one body. God's design. He places each where He wills.
God set the members 'as it hath pleased him'; many members, yet one body. God's design. He places each where He wills.
The eye cannot say to the hand, I have no need of thee; the feeble members are necessary. Mutual need. The least member is needful to the whole.
The eye cannot say to the hand, I have no need of thee; the feeble members are necessary. Mutual need. The least member is needful to the whole.
The eye cannot say to the hand, I have no need of thee; the feeble members are necessary. Mutual need. The least member is needful to the whole.
God tempered the body, 'that there should be no schism,' but care one for another. Shared feeling. The members suffer and rejoice together.
God tempered the body, 'that there should be no schism,' but care one for another. Shared feeling. The members suffer and rejoice together.
God tempered the body, 'that there should be no schism,' but care one for another. Shared feeling. The members suffer and rejoice together.
Ye are the body of Christ; God set apostles, prophets, teachers, and gifts in the church. Ordered church. Each has his appointed place and work.
Ye are the body of Christ; God set apostles, prophets, teachers, and gifts in the church. Ordered church. Each has his appointed place and work.
Are all apostles? 'covet earnestly the best gifts'; yet 'a more excellent way' I show. Best of all. Above every gift is the way of love.
